params.text:
Care done with people, not to them

Active participation means recognising each person as an active partner in their own care and daily life, rather than a passive recipient of it. When you support someone to do what they can for themselves, you protect their independence, skills and sense of worth.

  • Do with, not for: let someone wash the parts of their body they can reach, or choose and pass their own clothes, even if it takes longer.
  • Wellbeing: taking part builds confidence and can reduce the frustration and low mood that come with feeling helpless.

Involving people in their own care

People have the right to be involved in decisions about their support — a principle often summed up as ‘nothing about me without me’. Standard 7 asks you to actively encourage people to make choices and to help them take part in planning their care.

  • Offer real choices: what to wear, when to get up, what to eat, how to spend the day.
  • Share the care plan: involve the person and, where they wish, the people close to them in reviewing it.
  • Listen to feedback: act on what people tell you, and pass concerns you cannot resolve to your manager.

params.question: Mr Okafor can wash his own face and hands but finds it slow, and you are running behind. What best supports active participation?

answers.text: Give him the flannel and the time to wash what he can, and help only with what he cannot manage.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Doing with rather than for maintains his independence and dignity.

answers.text: Wash him quickly yourself so the round stays on time.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: This removes his independence and treats the schedule as more important than the person.

answers.text: Tell him he must be quicker tomorrow or you will have to do it for him.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: This pressures him and undermines his confidence and choice.

answers.text: Skip washing his face today to save time.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: This neglects his care and his right to be clean and comfortable.
